Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

what are the transcendental qualities of an uttama....rasika gaudiya vaisnava?

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2010-02-25 · 2 answers
Can you be an gaudiya vaisnava....an uttama adhikara ,[iskcon or gaudiya] archarya,when your personal realisations are in direct disagreement in substance and conclusions to the previous archaryas siddhanta and mutually accepted devotional culture?Second piont,Can anyone substantuate Narayanna maharajas claims....with sastra, his unique re-initation veiw!
Which is illustrated by this direct Quote.........
Narayana Maharaja Murwillumbah, Australia: Feb. 15, 2002 (Evening, Part 1):
"If the guru is following all the principles of bhakti, if he is in the line of his gurudeva, if he is chanting and remembering but he is not learned enough to give you Krishna-tattva, then dont give him up. With honor for him, take his permission, and go with his permission to associate with exalted Vaisnavas. And, if he does not give permission, you can consider that he is also among the kan-gurus meant for being given up."
user [343] · 2010-02-25
SGD this is the third thread that has been opened on Narayan Maharaja, and the second one by you, there is nothing genuine about your questions, they are politically loaded questions, if you feel you have answered all of these and convinced yourself what are you trying to achieve? Surely your time would be better spent, reading Srila Prabhupadas books, performing the positive rather than looking for the negative.

I dont think you have convinced one person in this forum SGD, because you are cutting and pasting quotes from subjects that you dont understand. Give it a rest move on, Gaura Purnima is about to start, blaspheming a great Vaisnava will not earn you respect from anyone and in the end it will be to your detriment.

You are in an absolute frenzy that appears to have taken over your life. There is a time even for a warrior to lay down his weapons, its time to celebrate the appearance of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, make it a time for peace and understanding between Viasnavas not hate and blasphemy.
user [488] · 2010-02-25
Yes, Sri Govinda Dasa, you keep spinning this subject so many times in so many ways that i too am
getting dizzy . Lets move on to another subject, something else close to your transcendental heart.
You can make it controversial if you like .
